Gels are stained with 0.5 µg/mL ethidium bromide for 30 min and visualized and photographed on a UV light box.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"erocl","text":"3-4 hour XZ-6E virus infected NC64A, pellets frozen at -80°C","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Prepare Buffer A: CviRI Buffer A by Irina Agarkova
Prepare Buffer B: CviRI Buffer B by Irina Agarkova
Prepare Buffer C: CviRI Buffer C by Irina Agarkova
Prepare Storage Buffer: CviRI Storage Buffer by Irina Agarkova
Prepare 1X CviRI Assay Buffer: 1X CviRI Assay Buffer by Irina Agarkova
Thaw the 3-4 hour XZ-6E virus-infected NC64A chlorella and suspend in MSK flasks with Buffer A. Suspend with 20 mL per flask per 1.0-1.5 X 10 11 infected cells.
Homogenize the cells in the MSK mechanical homogenizer with 15 gm of 0.3 mm glass beads at 4,000 rpm for 90 sec (2 X 45 sec) with CO 2 cooling. 00:01:30
Recover the homogenate to clean tubes.
Adjust the homogenate supernatant to 70% saturation with (NH 4 ) 2 SO 4 at 4°C with gentle stirring. Add the (NH 4 ) 2 SO 4 gradually.
Centrifuge the material in the Sorvall SS34 rotor at 10,000 rpm, 10 min, 4°C. 00:10:00
Suspend the pellets with Buffer A.
Centrifuge the material in the Sorvall SS34 rotor at 10,000 rpm, 10 min, 4°C. 00:10:00
Dilute the supernatant with 3-5 volumes of Buffer B to reduce the NaCl concentration.
Elute the Heparin-Sepharose column with Buffer B using a 0.2-1.2 M NaCl gradient.
Elute the Blue-Sepharose column (or Affi-Gel-Blue column) with Buffer B using a 0.2-2.0 M KOAc gradient.
Dilute the pooled fractions with 2 volumes of Buffer C to reduce the salt concentration.
Elute the Hydroxylapatite column with Buffer C using a 0-1.0 M KHPO 4 gradient.
Dilute the pooled fractions with 3-4 volumes of Buffer B.
Concentrate the pooled enzyme by dialysis overnight into storage buffer at 4°C. 18:00:00
Wash the glass beads 3X with 5 mL of Buffer A and combine with the homogenate.
Centrifuge the homogenate in the Sorvall SS34 rotor at 10,000 rpm,20 min, 4°C. 00:20:00
Save the supernatant.
Incubate at 4°C for 60-90 min without stirring. 01:30:00
Save the pellet.
Per mL of suspension add: 0.45 mL of 4 M NaCl and 0.45 mL of 28% PEG 8000 (heated to 65°C).
Mix gently by inversion for 5-10 min. 00:10:00
Save the supernatant. If there is small particulate matter in the supernatant, centrifuge a second time as before and save the supernatant.
Load the material overnight onto a Heparin-Sepharose column equilibrated with Buffer B in the cold room. 18:00:00
Assay the column fractions and pool the active fractions. CviRI Assay Conditions by Irina Agarkova
Dilute the pooled fractions with 3 volumes of Buffer B to reduce the salt concentration.
Load the material overnight onto a Blue-Sepharose column (or an Affi-Gel-Blue column) equilibrated with Buffer B in the cold room. 18:00:00
Assay the column fractions and pool the active fractions. CviRI Assay Conditions by Irina Agarkova
Load the material overnight onto an Hydroxylapatite column equilibrated with Buffer C in the cold room. 18:00:00
Assay the column fractions and pool the active fractions. CviRI Assay Conditions by Irina Agarkova
Load the material overnight onto a small Heparin-Sepharose column equilibrated with Buffer B. 18:00:00
Elute the column with Buffer B containing 2.0 M NaCl.
Collect small fractions (approximately 35 drops per fraction).
Assay the column fractions (only about the first 10-15 fractions) and pool the active fractions. CviRI Assay Conditions by Irina Agarkova
Store the enzyme at -20°C.
creator: Irina Agarkova
